content.description:
Displays children of an item as navigable slides — any item with attachments can act as a slide deck.

## Implementation

[slide-view](item://c0c0c0c0-0080-0000-0000-000000000000) — a Hob view for any item type (Atom).

## How It Works

1. All child items are rendered up front using their default view (attachment view overrides are ignored)
2. Each rendered child is wrapped in a hidden div
3. Navigation toggles `display:none` — instant, no async rendering on slide change
4. Keyboard: Arrow Left/Right, Home/End
5. Click: ‹ / › buttons pinned to left/right edges of the footer

## Zoom

A `−` / `+` control in the footer adjusts CSS `zoom` on the stage (50%–300% in 25% steps). Useful for presenting notes at a distance. The footer bar itself stays at normal size.

## Usage

Right-click any item with attachments → "View As..." → "Slides".

The default use case is notes-as-slides: create an item, attach child notes, view as slides. Each note renders with its default view (typically `note-view-readonly` with full markdown rendering, transclusions, etc.).

## Design Choices

- **Default views only** — ignores per-attachment view overrides stored by spatial canvas. Presentation wants clean read views, not whatever editing context the child was last in.
- **Pre-render all** — trades upfront cost for instant navigation. Fine for typical deck sizes (tens of slides, not hundreds).
- **CSS zoom over font-size** — scales everything uniformly including images, code blocks, and embedded content.

## Hob Patterns Demonstrated

- `for` (not `map`) for async iteration — `map` uses JS `Array.map` which doesn't await Hob fns
- `(dom-set! (dom-get el :style) :display val)` for nested property access — `dom-set!` doesn't support dotted paths
- `on-document!` for keyboard listeners with auto-cleanup
- `atom` + `deref` + `reset!` for local state (slide index, zoom level)
- `hiccup->dom+` with `:ref` for imperative DOM manipulation

## See Also

- [slide-view source](item://c0c0c0c0-0080-0000-0000-000000000000)
- [Structured Pages](item://c0c0c0c0-0073-0000-0000-000000000000) — related proposal for richer page composition
